---
title: "Alcohol and Mental Task Performance"
subtitle: "STATS 101B group project"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, fig.width = 6, fig.height = 4)
```

## Data

```{r}
cols <- c("subject", "alcohol", "difficulty", "score")
raw <- rbind(
  read.csv("../data/scores.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)[, cols],
  read.csv("../data/new_subjects.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)[, cols])
raw$alcohol    <- factor(raw$alcohol, levels = c(0, 1, 3))
raw$difficulty <- factor(raw$difficulty, levels = c("Easy", "Hard"))
raw$subject    <- factor(raw$subject)

# keep only blocks with the full 3x2 set of cells
complete <- names(which(table(raw$subject) == 6))
dat <- droplevels(raw[raw$subject %in% complete, ])
cat(nlevels(dat$subject), "complete blocks:", paste(levels(dat$subject), collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

## ANOVA

```{r}
fit <- aov(score ~ alcohol * difficulty + subject, data = dat)
summary(fit)
```

## Interaction

```{r}
with(dat, interaction.plot(alcohol, difficulty, score,
     xlab = "shots of vodka", ylab = "mean score", trace.label = "task"))
```

## Means

```{r}
round(tapply(dat$score, list(dat$alcohol, dat$difficulty), mean), 2)
TukeyHSD(fit, "alcohol")
```

## Diagnostics

```{r}
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
qqnorm(residuals(fit)); qqline(residuals(fit))
plot(fitted(fit), residuals(fit), xlab = "Fitted", ylab = "Residuals")
abline(h = 0, lty = 2)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
shapiro.test(residuals(fit))
```
